calorifiers_cylinders.name: Calorifiers & Hot Water Cylinders
calorifiers_cylinders.function: Store hot water — always insulated to minimise standing heat loss.

factory_insulated.description: Equipment supplied with factory-applied insulation (sprayed PU foam or rigid foam jacket).
factory_insulated.action: Note as "factory pre-insulated" in takeoff. No site-applied insulation required.
factory_insulated.check: Confirm factory insulation meets spec thickness and performance. Some specs require ADDITIONAL insulation over factory finish.
factory_insulated.flag_if: Spec insulation thickness exceeds factory insulation — additional site-applied insulation may be needed.

site_applied.description: Insulation applied on site around bare vessel.

materials.mineral_wool: Mineral wool slab or mat, wired on, with aluminium or PVC cladding. Standard for LTHW calorifiers.
materials.phenolic: Phenolic foam slab — thinner for equivalent performance. Space-constrained plant rooms.
materials.spray_foam: Spray-applied polyurethane foam — fast, seamless. Often for large vessels. Applied by specialist.

typical_thickness.enhanced: 75mm (where reduced standing loss required, Part L compliance)
typical_thickness.high_performance: 100mm (where spec demands very low standing loss)

site_applied.finishing: Aluminium cladding (standard), stainless steel (wet areas), PVC (internal/lower cost).

surface_area_calc.vertical_cylinder: SA = π × D × H + 2 × π × (D/2)² = π × D × (H + D/2). Includes top and bottom caps.
surface_area_calc.horizontal_cylinder: SA = π × D × L + 2 × π × (D/2)². Includes end caps.
surface_area_calc.note: Use EXTERNAL dimensions of the vessel (before insulation). Equipment schedule should state dimensions.

calorifiers_cylinders.count_rule: If factory insulated: count as 1nr with note "factory insulated — verify meets spec". If site insulated: measure m² surface area + cladding m².
associated_items: Removable sections at inspection hatches and connections

cold_water_tank.name: Cold Water Storage Tank (CWST)
cold_water_tank.material: Elastomeric foam (closed-cell, integral VB) or phenolic foam slabs with vapour barrier.
cold_water_tank.thickness: 25-50mm (depends on ambient temperature and L8 requirement to keep below 20°C)
cold_water_tank.vapour_barrier: ESSENTIAL — cold water tank in warm plant room will condensate without VB.
cold_water_tank.note: Lid must be insulated too — heat rises and warms water through uninsulated lid.
cold_water_tank.flag_if: Cold water tank without insulation specification — flag for L8 compliance.

buffer_vessel.name: Buffer Vessel / Thermal Store
buffer_vessel.material: Mineral wool slab or phenolic, with cladding.
buffer_vessel.note: Buffer vessels store heat — insulation prevents heat loss. Often factory pre-insulated with PU foam.

expansion_vessel.name: Expansion Vessel
expansion_vessel.material: Usually factory pre-insulated (if insulated at all).
expansion_vessel.note: Small expansion vessels are often NOT insulated. Large pressurisation units may be insulated. Check spec.
expansion_vessel.flag_if: Large expansion vessel or pressurisation unit without insulation mentioned — check spec.

header_tank.name: Feed & Expansion Tank (F&E)
header_tank.material: Mineral wool or phenolic, with cladding. Lid insulated.
header_tank.note: Open-vented systems. Tank is usually in cold space (roof void). Frost protection may be required — check.

brine_tank.name: Brine / Chemical Tank
brine_tank.material: Specialist — depends on contents temperature and chemical compatibility.
brine_tank.note: Specialist application. Check spec carefully for material compatibility.

measurement.includes: Sides + top + bottom (unless bottom is resting on insulated plinth).
measurement.note: Rectangular tanks: SA = 2(L×H + W×H) + L×W (top) + L×W (bottom if insulated). Cylindrical: see calorifier formula.

pumps.name: Pump Insulation
pumps.rule: Pumps on heating and chilled water systems should be insulated. Check spec — not all specs require pump insulation.

removable_box.name: Removable Pump Insulation Box
removable_box.description: Pre-fabricated removable insulation enclosure. Allows pump maintenance without destroying insulation.
removable_box.note: Removable boxes are measured by the insulation manufacturer from site dimensions. Supplied as bespoke items.

removable_jacket.name: Removable Insulation Jacket / Mattress
removable_jacket.description: Flexible insulation jacket with Velcro/toggle fastening, shaped to fit pump casing.
removable_jacket.note: More expensive than permanent insulation but essential for pumps that need regular maintenance.

permanent.name: Permanent Insulation (Site Applied)
permanent.description: Mineral wool or elastomeric wrapped around pump casing. NOT recommended — prevents maintenance.
permanent.note: Rarely specified because it must be destroyed for pump maintenance. Removable type preferred.

by_system.lthw: Insulate — hot pump casing loses heat. Removable box/jacket standard.
by_system.chw: Insulate — cold pump casing causes condensation. Elastomeric or removable jacket with VB. CRITICAL.
by_system.dhw_secondary: Insulate — hot water secondary return pump. Removable jacket.
by_system.note: Cold system pumps (ChW) MUST have vapour-sealed insulation to prevent condensation dripping.

flag_if: Pump on chilled water system without insulation — flag condensation risk.
Pump insulation specified as permanent (not removable) — flag maintenance access concern.

valves_flanges.name: Valve & Flange Insulation
valves_flanges.rule: Valves and flanges on insulated pipework systems are insulated. Refer to KB-I01 for multiplier rates. Count as nr.

valve_box_removable.name: Removable Valve Insulation Box
valve_box_removable.description: Pre-formed or fabricated insulation box with hinged lid or Velcro closure. Allows valve operation without removing insulation.
valve_box_removable.sizing: Note pipe size and valve type — valve box size varies significantly.
valve_box_removable.rate_guide: Allow 1.5× the linear pipe insulation rate per valve (KB-I01 reference).
valve_box_removable.note: Isolation valves that need frequent access should ALWAYS have removable boxes, not permanent insulation.

valve_permanent.name: Permanent Valve Insulation
valve_permanent.description: Site-fabricated insulation moulded around valve body. Permanent — must be cut to access valve.
valve_permanent.measurement: nr or included in linear pipe rate with multiplier.
valve_permanent.note: Cheaper than removable boxes but prevents easy valve maintenance. Check spec preference.

flange_box.name: Flange Insulation Box / Cover
flange_box.description: Removable insulation cover for flanged joints. Two-piece with clips or Velcro.
flange_box.rate_guide: Allow 2× the linear pipe insulation rate per flange pair (KB-I01 reference).
flange_box.note: Flanges need removable insulation for bolt inspection and joint maintenance.

large_bore_flanges.name: Large Bore Flange Insulation (>DN150)
large_bore_flanges.description: Large flanges may be measured as m² surface area rather than nr item due to the significant surface.
large_bore_flanges.measurement: m² for flanges >DN150, or nr with specific pricing.
large_bore_flanges.note: Large bore flange insulation is a significant item — more material and labour than small bore.

strainer_box.name: Strainer Insulation Box
strainer_box.description: Removable insulation box for Y-strainer or basket strainer. MUST be removable for cleaning.
strainer_box.note: Strainer boxes are ALWAYS removable. Strainers need frequent cleaning — permanent insulation is never acceptable.

valves_flanges.cold_system_note: Valve and flange insulation on cold/chilled systems MUST be vapour-sealed. Uninsulated valves on chilled pipework are cold bridges → condensation → dripping → damage. This is a common site problem — flag if not addressed.

brazed_phe.name: Brazed Plate Heat Exchanger
brazed_phe.insulation: Removable insulation jacket. Factory-sized to fit specific model.
brazed_phe.note: Brazed PHEs are compact — jacket covers the plate pack. Some are factory pre-insulated.

gasket_phe.name: Gasketed Plate Heat Exchanger
gasket_phe.insulation: Removable jacket or permanent insulation to frame and plate pack.
gasket_phe.measurement: nr or m² — depends on size.
gasket_phe.note: Gasket PHEs need plate removal for cleaning. Insulation MUST allow access to plate pack.

shell_and_tube.name: Shell & Tube Heat Exchanger
shell_and_tube.insulation: Site-applied mineral wool with cladding. End covers removable.
shell_and_tube.note: Larger item — measure surface area. End covers need removable insulation for tube cleaning.

cladding.rule: Cladding is the external protective finish over insulation. It is a SEPARATE item from the insulation itself. Always note cladding type if specified.

aluminium.name: Aluminium Cladding (Stucco Embossed)
aluminium.description: Pre-formed or site-bent aluminium sheet over insulation. Pop-riveted or banded.
aluminium.finish: Stucco embossed (textured) standard. Smooth (plain) available. Mill finish or painted.
aluminium.use: MOST COMMON cladding for commercial M&E. Plant rooms, risers, visible areas, external pipework.
aluminium.measurement: m² (surface area of cladding — same as insulation m² plus overlap allowance).
aluminium.accessories: Self-tapping screws, pop rivets, corner pieces, end caps, saddle supports.
aluminium.note: Aluminium cladding is a significant cost item — often 30-50% of total insulation cost.

stainless_steel.name: Stainless Steel Cladding
stainless_steel.description: Stainless steel sheet (304 or 316 grade) over insulation.
stainless_steel.use: Food production, pharmaceutical, swimming pool plant, coastal/corrosive environments, high-hygiene areas.
stainless_steel.note: Significantly more expensive than aluminium (3-5×). Only specified where aluminium is not suitable.

pvc.name: PVC Cladding / PVC Jacket
pvc.description: Pre-formed PVC sections that clip or wrap around insulated pipe.
pvc.use: Internal pipework where appearance matters but cost must be controlled. Often for visible pipework in corridors, offices.
pvc.measurement: m (linear) by pipe size, or m².
pvc.note: Cheaper than aluminium. Lighter weight. Not suitable for external use (UV degradation). Not fire rated — check if area requires non-combustible cladding.

canvas.name: Canvas Finish (Scrim + Paint)
canvas.description: Glass cloth (scrim) applied over insulation with adhesive, then painted.
canvas.use: Plant rooms, back-of-house, where basic finish is acceptable and cost must be minimised.
canvas.note: Cheapest finish option. Suitable for concealed or plant room applications. Not weather-resistant. Not vandal-resistant.

self_finish.name: Self-Finish (Foil Faced)
self_finish.description: Insulation is foil-faced and left with foil as the final finish. No additional cladding.
self_finish.use: Concealed areas (ceiling voids, risers where not visible). Where insulation is hidden by ceiling tiles or duct casing.
self_finish.measurement: No additional cladding item — included in insulation cost.
self_finish.note: Cheapest option — no separate cladding cost. Only suitable where insulation is not visible, not subject to damage, and not external.

selection_guide.external: Aluminium (standard) or stainless steel (corrosive/coastal). Weather-resistant fixing method.
selection_guide.ceiling_void: Self-finish (foil-faced) — not visible. No cladding needed.
selection_guide.corridors_visible: PVC cladding or aluminium — neat appearance.
selection_guide.wet_areas: Stainless steel or sealed aluminium — moisture-resistant.

cladding.measurement_rule: Cladding is measured as m² (same area as insulation + 5-10% for laps, joints, and fittings). Cladding accessories (rivets, screws, bands, corner pieces) are typically included in the m² rate.

general: Equipment insulation is measured in m² (surface area) for site-applied insulation.
Pre-insulated equipment: count as nr with note "factory pre-insulated — verify meets spec".
Removable items (valve boxes, pump jackets): count as nr.
Cladding: measured separately from insulation as m².
Use equipment schedule dimensions to calculate surface area.
If dimensions not available from schedule or drawing, flag as "equipment dimensions required for insulation takeoff".

vertical_cylinder.description: Cylinder sides + top cap + bottom cap.

horizontal_cylinder.example: Buffer vessel: D=0.8m, L=2.0m → SA = π × 0.8 × 2.0 + π × 0.16 = 5.53 m²

rectangular_tank.note: Bottom may not be insulated if tank sits on insulated plinth. Check.

pre_insulated_rule.rule: If equipment is factory pre-insulated, note in takeoff but DO NOT add site-applied insulation unless spec requires additional insulation over factory finish.
pre_insulated_rule.check: Always verify: (a) is it actually pre-insulated? (b) does factory insulation meet spec thickness? (c) does cladding still need to be applied over factory insulation?

waste_factor.equipment: 10% — for cutting, shaping around connections, nozzles, and supports.
waste_factor.cladding: 12% — for laps, joints, corner pieces, and shaping around protrusions.
waste_factor.cross_ref: See KB-C02 for general waste factor rules.

flag_triggers: Equipment shown on drawings but not on equipment schedule — flag. Cannot determine dimensions for insulation measurement.
Equipment schedule exists but no insulation specification stated — flag. Insulation type, thickness, and finish unknown.
Cold equipment (ChW buffer, CWS tank) in warm environment without insulation — flag condensation risk.
High temperature equipment (steam, HTHW >100°C) — specialist insulation required (calcium silicate or high-temp mineral wool). Flag if standard insulation specified.
Pre-insulated equipment shown but spec requires additional insulation — flag for clarification.
Removable insulation not specified for items needing regular maintenance access (pumps, strainers, control valves) — flag.
Cladding type not stated — flag. Cladding is a significant cost and appearance factor.
Equipment in external location without weather-resistant cladding specified — flag.
Equipment insulation shown on hot system but not on cold system in same plant room — flag. Cold system also needs insulation (condensation).
Large vessel (>500 litres) without dimensions on schedule — flag. Cannot calculate surface area.
Insulation to existing equipment specified — flag. May need removal of old insulation first (asbestos risk if pre-2000 building).
Valve insulation on chilled water system not specified — flag. Uninsulated valves = cold bridges = condensation = dripping.
